abstract: This article examines the proposed reform of Polish water law, focusing on the fundamental objectives of the projected new Water Law Act. Key areas of analysis include the reorganization of administrative competencies, the separation of water infrastructure management from broader water resource governance, the implementation of river-basin-oriented policies, and the simplification of financing mechanisms. By comparing existing regulations with the planned legislative changes, the authors highlight systemic failures in the current legal framework and investigate their underlying causes. Ultimately, the paper evaluates whether the proposed legal solutions are equipped to effectively address these long-standing issues and provide a more coherent, sustainable approach to water management in Poland.
